quality.rationale: 通过Lie括号对易关系与点态矢量分解证明Killing向量场的局部相关与全局独立，并结合三维旋转群的参数自由度给出清晰的几何物理解释

origin.question: 10.4. In Euclidean 3-space show that the 3 Killing vectors describing rotations around the $x, y$ , and $z$ axes are linearly dependent at any given point but that no constant coefficient combination of them is zero. Show that the generators of the rotation group $O(3)$ are thus 2-surface forming, even though the group is 3-dimensional. Explain.
origin.answer: At any given point, the three Killing vectors are linearly dependent and span a 2-dimensional tangent plane. Globally they are linearly independent and span a 3-dimensional space. The generators form 2-surfaces because 2-spheres are intrinsically 2-dimensional, while specifying their orientation requires 3 independent parameters (e.g., Euler angles).
origin.solution: Solution 10.4. The three Killing vectors describing rotations are just the angular momentum operators, $\mathbf{J}_{\mathbf{z}} = \mathbf{x}\partial_{\mathbf{y}} - \mathbf{y}\partial_{\mathbf{x}}$ , etc. (See Problem 10.1.) At a point $(\mathbf{x}_0,\mathbf{y}_0,\mathbf{z}_0)$$$\begin{array}{l}J_{z} = (- y_{0}, x_{0}, 0) \\\mathbf {J_{y}} = (z_{0}, 0, - \mathbf {x}_{0}) \\\mathrm {J}_{\mathbf {x}} = \left(0, - z_{0}, y_{0}\right), \\\end{array}$$so $\mathbf{J_y} = -(z_0 / y_0)\mathbf{J_z} - (x_0 / y_0)\mathbf{J_x}$ and the three vectors only span a 2-dimensional surface at the point.Suppose, however, that for some constants $a$ and $b$$$a J_{x} + b J_{y} + J_{z} = 0$$were true at every point, then we would have$$a \left[ J_{x}, J_{y}\right] + b \left[ J_{y}, J_{y}\right] + \left[ J_{z}, J_{y}\right] = 0.$$This requires $\mathbf{J}_{\mathbf{z}} \propto \mathbf{J}_{\mathbf{x}}$ , a result which is obviously false. Thus, over the whole sphere, $\mathbf{J}_{\mathbf{x}}, \mathbf{J}_{\mathbf{y}}, \mathbf{J}_{\mathbf{z}}$ span a 3-dimensional space. The explanation is that 2-spheres are 2-dimensional, but orientations of 2-spheres are 3-dimensional (e.g. the 3 Euler angles).
